mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth reflects contemporaneous manufacturing and utility output; sustained expansion raises corporate revenue expectations and capacity utilization, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and collateral values that tightens household credit and reduces construction employment, transmitting a negative wealth effect to equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,500.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy, as measured by FEDFUNDS above 5%, and contracting industrial production, measured by INDPRO falling below its trailing 3-month average, jointly squeeze corporate margins through high real financing costs amid falling output; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0 and INDPRO below trailing 3-month average
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances produce negative 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 45% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) versus single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) extends the expected duration of elevated discount rates and reduces present values of long-duration cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns; this effect is absent or reversed in easing cycles.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS > 5.0, positive 20-day SPX returns follow CPIAUCSL acceleration in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe in tightening cycles is not significantly lower than in easing cycles (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor income and consumer confidence, triggering downward earnings revisions and risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: UNRATE trigger events are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 45% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) captures real-time consumer spending velocity; higher spending flows directly into top-line revenue for consumer-facing firms and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured from vintage releases proxies aggregate demand expansion; above-median vintage growth rates reflect strong macro momentum that gets priced into equities over the subsequent quarter, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns follow above-median GDPC1 growth in fewer than 55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that is more than 50 basis points above its trailing 12-month vintage average signals an unexpectedly restrictive monetary stance that tightens financial conditions, reduces leverage, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS trigger episodes are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 45% of cases.
